session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the existing gateway patterns in this codebase. Specifically:
1. Look at the Shell gateway as a simple 3-file reference: - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/__init__.py - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/abc.py - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/real.py - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/fake.py
2. Look at how run_subprocess_with_context works: - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/subprocess_utils.py
3. Look at ErkContext: - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py (the for_test method and field declarations)
4. Look at how Real gateways are wired in production: - src/erk/core/context.py
5. Look at existing tests structure: - Find a test file for a simple gateway like shell or codespace for patterns
Give me the full content of all these files so I can understand the patterns.
session.assistant: I'll help you understand the gateway patterns in this codebase. Let me first check the documentation, then explore the specific files you've requested.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Gateway Documentation 5→ 6→- **[codespace-gateway.md](codespace-gateway.md)** — adding a new codespace SSH operation to the gateway, deciding whether a new gateway needs dry-run and printing implementations, testing code that uses os.execvp or NoReturn methods, choosing between exec_ssh_interactive and run_ssh_command 7→- **[codespace-registry.md](codespace-registry.md)** — working with GitHub Codespace registration or lookup, adding a gateway that separates read-only ABC from mutation functions, choosing between ABC methods vs standalone functions for gateway operations 8→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→--- 2→title: Gateway Hierarchy 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding gateway relationships" 5→ - "deciding which gateway to use for an operation" 6→ - "understanding BranchManager abstraction" 7→ - "understanding GraphiteDisabled sentinel pattern" 8→ - "querying both Graphite and GitHub for completeness" 9→ - "understanding dual-source patterns" 10→last_audited: "2026-02-16 04:53 PT" 11→audit_result: clean 12→--- 13→ 14→# Gateway Hierarchy 15→ 16→Overview of erk's gateway architecture showing the layered abstraction from low-level primitives to high-level orchestration. 17→ 18→## Gateway Layers 19→ 20→``` 21→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 22→│ Higher-Level Abstractions │ 23→│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ 24→│ │ BranchManager ││ 25→│ │ Dual-mode branch operations (Graphite or plain Git) ││ 26→│ │ - get_pr_for_branch() - Uses Graphite cache OR GitHub API ││ 27→│ │ - create_branch() - Uses gt create OR git branch ││ 28→│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│ 29→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 30→ │
... [185 lines omitted] ...
100→3. **Clear errors**: Mutating operations raise `GraphiteDisabledError` with helpful messages 114→ # Mutating ops raise helpful errors 116→ raise GraphiteDisabledError(self.reason)

1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "creating a new gateway ABC" 11→ warning: "Default is 3-file pattern (abc.py, real.py, fake.py). Only add dry_run.py if the gateway participates in a user-facing --dry-run feature. Most gateways do not." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to a dry-run-enabled gateway ABC (Git, LocalGitHub, Graphite)" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to a 3-file gateway ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 3 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from all implementation files simultaneously (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all implementations import the new types. For 4-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py. For 3-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [782 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All implementations must be updated (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests." 33→ warning: "Gateway names must reflect the TOOL being wrapped, not the execution mechanism. Use LocalGitHub for gh calls, Git for git calls, CmuxGateway for cmux calls, PromptExecutor for claude calls. A mechanism-named gateway is just moving the mock up one layer without gaining abstraction." 35→ warning: "Must also wire into ErkContext: add field to context.py dataclass, add parameter to for_test(), and wire Real* in production factory (src/erk/core/context.py). See 'New Gateway: ErkContext Wiring' section." 44→**Exception: erk-dev** (`packages/erk-dev/`) is developer tooling and is exempt from these rules. Direct `subprocess.run` with git commands is acceptable in erk-dev since it doesn't need gateway abstractions for its own operations. 172→When designing error handling for a new gateway method, the key question is: **does the caller continue after the failure?** 174→This checklist helps you choose between discriminated unions and exceptions. For full pattern documentation, see [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md). 176→### Decision: Discriminated Union or Exception? 180→| Does at least one caller branch on the error and continue with different logic? | Discriminated union | Exception | 181→| Does the error carry domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message`? | Discriminated union | Exception | 182→| Do all callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop)? | Exception | Discriminated union | 186→- [ ] At least one caller branches on the error and continues with different logic 187→- [ ] Error type carries domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message` 188→- [ ] Error type implements the `NonIdealState` protocol (`error_type` + `message` properties) from `erk_shared.non_ideal_state` 192→### Checklist: Use Exceptions When 194→- [ ] All callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop) 195→- [ ] No caller inspects error type or branches on error content 196→- [ ] Use `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary 202→All discriminated union error types must implement the `NonIdealState` protocol from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. The protocol requires two read-only properties: `error_type` (machine-readable classification string) and `message` (human-readable description). 217→**Methods using discriminated unions** (callers branch on error): 221→| `merge_pr` | `MergeResult \| MergeError` | GitHub | 222→| `push_to_remote` | `PushResult \| PushError` | Git remote_ops | 223→| `pull_rebase` | `PullRebaseResult \| PullRebaseError` | Git remote_ops | 225→| `submit_branch` | `SubmitBranchResult \| SubmitBranchError` | BranchManager | 227→**Methods using exceptions** (all callers terminate): 229→| Method | Exception | Why | 231→| Worktree add/remove | `RuntimeError` | No caller inspects error content; all terminate identically | 232→| HTTP operations | `HttpError` | Generic transport failure; callers just surface the message | 236→When changing an existing gateway method's return type (e.g., converting from exception-based to discriminated union), follow this comprehensive migration pattern: 243→ - [ ] `real.py` - Return appropriate error types for failure cases 245→ - [ ] `dry_run.py` - Return appropriate success/error based on mode (4-file gateways only) 247→5. [ ] Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 252→**PR #6294** (`merge_pr: bool | str` → `MergeResult | MergeError`): 256→- Updated tests to use `isinstance(result, MergeError)` 274→Some resources benefit from existence-check methods that enable Look Before You Leap validation. This pattern prevents cryptic errors when fetching non-existent resources. 282→- When error messages from `get_X()` on missing resources are unclear 305→ user_output(f"Error: Issue #{issue_number} not found") 380→6. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Update any tests that assumed failure on missing resource 610→The worktree sub-gateway follows the same 4-file pattern with methods: `list_worktrees()`, `add_worktree()`, `move_worktree()`, `remove_worktree()`, `prune_worktrees()`, `find_worktree_for_branch()`. Note: Worktree operations use exceptions (RuntimeError), not discriminated unions. 697→**Partial removal causes type checker errors** - if you remove from abc.py but forget another implementation, the type checker will complain about missing abstract method implementations. 715→1. Run type checker (`ty`) to verify no abstract method errors 772→├── abc.py # push_to_remote() -> PushResult | PushError 774→├── fake.py # Constructor-injected errors, mutation tracking 780→- **Discriminated union returns**: Methods return `Success | Error` instead of raising 781→- **Error boundary in real.py**: `try/except RuntimeError` converts to `PushError` 782→- **Fake configuration**: `FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(...))` injects failures 805→- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) - Where exceptions become discriminated unions

This abstraction 5→enables dependency injection for testing without mock.patch. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→from pathlib import Path 10→ 11→ 12→def detect_shell_from_env(shell_env: str) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: 13→ """Detect shell type and config file from SHELL environment value. 14→ 15→ Args: 16→ shell_env: Value of SHELL environment variable (e.g., "/bin/zsh") 17→ 18→ Returns: 19→ Tuple of (shell_name, rc_file_path) or None if unsupported shell 20→ """ 21→ if not shell_env: 22→ return None 23→ 24→ shell_name = Path(shell_env).name 25→ 26→ if shell_name == "bash": 27→ return ("bash", Path.home() / ".bashrc") 28→ if shell_name == "zsh": 29→ return ("zsh", Path.home() / ".zshrc") 30→ if shell_name == "fish":

1→"""Real implementation of Shell using system environment.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→import os 5→import shutil 6→import subprocess 7→from pathlib import Path 8→ 9→from erk_shared.gateway.shell.abc import Shell, detect_shell_from_env 10→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import ( 11→ build_claude_subprocess_env, 12→ run_subprocess_with_context, 13→) 14→ 15→ 16→class RealShell(Shell): 17→ """Production implementation using system environment and PATH.""" 18→ 19→ def detect_shell(self) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: 20→ """Detect current shell from SHELL environment variable. 21→ 22→ Implementation details: 23→ - Reads $SHELL environment variable 24→ - Extracts shell name from path (e.g., /bin/bash -> bash) 25→ - Maps to appropriate RC file location 26→ """ 27→ shell_env = os.environ.get("SHELL", "") 28→ return detect_shell_from_env(shell_env) 29→ 30→ def get_installed_tool_path(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None:

1→"""Simple subprocess utilities for erk-shared. 2→ 3→This module provides basic subprocess execution for GitHub CLI commands. 4→It's intentionally minimal to avoid pulling in complex dependencies. 5→""" 6→ 7→import logging 8→import os 9→import subprocess 10→import time 11→from collections.abc import Sequence 12→from pathlib import Path 13→from typing import IO, Any 14→ 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.retry import ( 16→ RETRY_DELAYS, 17→ RetriesExhausted, 18→ RetryRequested, 19→ with_retries, 20→) 21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.transient_errors import is_transient_error 22→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 23→ 24→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) 25→ 26→ 27→def copied_env_for_git_subprocess() -> dict[str, str]: 28→ """Return a copy of the environment for git subprocess calls. 29→ 30→ Sets GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 so git commands fail fast with an error
... [225 lines omitted] ...
87→ """Execute subprocess with enriched error reporting for integration layer. 89→ Wraps subprocess.run() to catch CalledProcessError and re-raise as RuntimeError 109→ RuntimeError: If command fails or times out, with enriched error context 110→ FileNotFoundError: If command binary is not found 140→ error_msg = f"Timed out after {timeout}s trying to {operation_context}" 141→ error_msg += f"
Command: {cmd_str}" 142→ raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e 144→ except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: 146→ error_msg = f"Failed to {operation_context}" 147→ error_msg += f"
Command: {cmd_str}" 148→ error_msg += f"
Exit code: {e.returncode}" 154→ error_msg += f"
stdout: {stdout_stripped}" 160→ error_msg += f"
stderr: {stderr_stripped}" 162→ raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e 164→ except FileNotFoundError as e: 166→ error_msg = f"Command not found while trying to {operation_context}: {cmd[0]}" 167→ error_msg += f"
Full command: {cmd_str}" 168→ raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e 173→# transient errors, but each attempt won't hang indefinitely. 188→ RuntimeError: If command fails or times out, with enriched error context 189→ FileNotFoundError: If gh is not installed 210→ """Execute gh command with automatic retry on transient network errors. 213→ Transient errors (network timeouts, connection failures) trigger automatic 226→ RuntimeError: If command fails after all retries, or with non-transient error 227→ FileNotFoundError: If gh is not installed 234→ except RuntimeError as e: 235→ if is_transient_error(str(e)): 243→ msg = f"GitHub command failed after retries: {result.reason}" 244→ raise RuntimeError(msg)

1→"""Unified context for erk and erk-kits operations. 2→ 3→This module provides ErkContext - the unified context that holds all dependencies 4→for erk and erk-kits operations. 5→ 6→The ABCs for erk-specific services (PromptExecutor, ConfigStore, ScriptWriter, 7→PlanListService) are defined in erk_shared.core, enabling 8→proper type hints without circular imports. Real implementations remain in erk. 9→""" 10→ 11→from __future__ import annotations 12→ 13→from dataclasses import dataclass 14→from pathlib import Path 15→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 16→ 17→from erk_shared.context.types import ( 18→ GlobalConfig, 19→ LoadedConfig, 20→ NoRepoSentinel, 21→ RepoContext, 22→) 23→ 24→if TYPE_CHECKING: 25→ from erk.artifacts.paths import ErkPackageInfo 26→ from erk_shared.core.health_check_runner import HealthCheckRunner 27→ from erk_shared.core.objective_list_service import ObjectiveListService 28→ 29→from erk_shared.core.plan_list_service import PlanListService 30→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor

1→"""Tests for FakeShell test infrastructure. 2→ 3→These tests verify that FakeShell correctly simulates shell operations, 4→providing reliable test doubles for CLI tests. 5→""" 6→ 7→from pathlib import Path 8→ 9→from tests.fakes.shell import FakeShell 10→ 11→ 12→def test_fake_shell_ops_initialization() -> None: 13→ """Test that FakeShell initializes with empty state.""" 14→ ops = FakeShell() 15→ 16→ shell = ops.detect_shell() 17→ assert shell is None 18→ 19→ tool = ops.get_installed_tool_path("any-tool") 20→ assert tool is None 21→ 22→ 23→def test_fake_shell_ops_detect_shell() -> None: 24→ """Test that detect_shell returns pre-configured shell type.""" 25→ shell_config = ("bash", Path.home() / ".bashrc") 26→ ops = FakeShell(detected_shell=shell_config) 27→ 28→ result = ops.detect_shell() 29→ 30→ assert result == ("bash", Path.home() / ".bashrc")

1→"""Integration tests for Shell. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealShell correctly integrates with the system 4→environment and external tools using actual system state. 5→ 6→Shell-specific detection logic is tested in tests/unit/test_shell_ops_functions.py 7→via the detect_shell_from_env() function. 8→""" 9→ 10→import os 11→ 12→from erk.core.shell import RealShell 13→ 14→ 15→def test_real_shell_ops_detect_shell_with_current_environment(): 16→ """Test that RealShell.detect_shell() works with actual current environment. 17→ 18→ This integration test verifies RealShell correctly reads from the 19→ actual SHELL environment variable and returns valid shell information. 20→ The test passes regardless of which shell is running it (or None if unsupported). 21→ """ 22→ ops = RealShell() 23→ result = ops.detect_shell() 24→ 25→ # If SHELL env var is set and supported, should return valid tuple 26→ shell_env = os.environ.get("SHELL", "") 27→ if shell_env and any(s in shell_env for s in ["bash", "zsh", "fish"]): 28→ assert result is not None 29→ shell_name, rc_file = result 30→ assert shell_name in ["bash", "zsh", "fish"]

1→"""Shell detection and tool availability operations. 2→ 3→This module provides abstraction over shell-specific operations like detecting 4→the current shell and checking if command-line tools are installed. Perfect! Now let me get a summary. I've gathered all the key gateway pattern files. Let me create a comprehensive report:
## Gateway Patterns in Erk: Complete Reference
I've analyzed the gateway architecture in this codebase. Here's the complete reference for understanding the patterns:
### Documentation Foundation
Before diving into code, I found comprehensive gateway documentation:
- **Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md`) - The authoritative reference for gateway patterns - **Gateway Hierarchy** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-hierarchy.md`) - Conceptual overview of how gateways relate - **Gateway Index** (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/gateway/`) - Specific gateway documentation
---
## 1. Shell Gateway: 3-File Reference Pattern
The Shell gateway is an excellent simple reference. Here's the complete anatomy:
### ABC (Contract Definition) **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/abc.py`
```python class Shell(ABC): @abstractmethod def detect_shell(self) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: """Detect current shell and return configuration file path.""" ...
@abstractmethod def get_installed_tool_path(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: """Check if command-line tool is installed and in PATH.""" ...
@abstractmethod def get_tool_version(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: """Get version string of installed CLI tool.""" ...
@abstractmethod def spawn_subshell( self, *, cwd: Path, shell_path: str, command: str, env: dict[str, str], ) -> int: """Spawn interactive subshell that executes command then stays open.""" ... ```
**Key patterns in ABC:** - Pure abstract methods with `...` (ellipsis) - Comprehensive docstrings with Args, Returns, Examples - Read-only methods (all query operations) - One mutation method (`spawn_subshell`)
### Real Implementation (Production) **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/real.py`
```python class RealShell(Shell): def detect_shell(self) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: shell_env = os.environ.get("SHELL", "") return detect_shell_from_env(shell_env)
def get_installed_tool_path(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: return shutil.which(tool_name)
def get_tool_version(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: tool_path = shutil.which(tool_name) if tool_path is None: return None
try: result = subprocess.run( [tool_name, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True, check=False, timeout=5, ) version_output = result.stdout.strip() or result.stderr.strip() return version_output if version_output else None except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError): return None
def spawn_subshell( self, *, cwd: Path, shell_path: str, command: str, env: dict[str, str], ) -> int: shell_name = Path(shell_path).name
if shell_name in ("bash", "zsh", "sh"): shell_args = [ shell_path, "-c", f"{command}; exec {shell_path} -i", ] else: shell_args = [ shell_path, "-c", f"{command}; exec {shell_path}", ]
merged_env = os.environ.copy() merged_env.update(env)
result = subprocess.run( shell_args, cwd=cwd, env=merged_env, )
return result.returncode ```
**Key patterns in Real:** - Uses subprocess for actual operations - Uses `shutil.which()` for PATH lookups - Wraps `subprocess.run()` with proper error handling - Sets up environment correctly before spawning - Returns concrete values (exit codes, paths, etc.)
### Fake Implementation (Testing) **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/fake.py`
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class SpawnSubshellCall: """Record of a spawn_subshell call for test assertions.""" cwd: Path shell_path: str command: str env: dict[str, str]
class FakeShell(Shell): def __init__( self, *, detected_shell: tuple[str, Path] | None = None, installed_tools: dict[str, str] | None = None, tool_versions: dict[str, str] | None = None, claude_extraction_raises: bool = False, extraction_plan_url: str | None = None, subshell_exit_code: int = 0, ) -> None: self._detected_shell = detected_shell self._installed_tools = installed_tools or {} self._tool_versions = tool_versions or {} self._extraction_calls: list[Path] = [] self._claude_extraction_raises = claude_extraction_raises self._extraction_plan_url = extraction_plan_url self._subshell_exit_code = subshell_exit_code self._subshell_calls: list[SpawnSubshellCall] = []
def detect_shell(self) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: return self._detected_shell
def get_installed_tool_path(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: return self._installed_tools.get(tool_name)
def get_tool_version(self, tool_name: str) -> str | None: return self._tool_versions.get(tool_name)
def spawn_subshell( self, *, cwd: Path, shell_path: str, command: str, env: dict[str, str], ) -> int: self._subshell_calls.append( SpawnSubshellCall( cwd=cwd, shell_path=shell_path, command=command, env=env.copy(), ) ) return self._subshell_exit_code
@property def subshell_calls(self) -> list[SpawnSubshellCall]: """For test assertions.""" return self._subshell_calls.copy() ```
**Key patterns in Fake:** - **Constructor injection**: All test data passed in `__init__` - **Frozen dataclass**: For call recording (`SpawnSubshellCall`) - **Mutation tracking**: Lists/sets to record what was called - **Read-only properties**: Return copies of mutation tracking for assertions - **Sensible defaults**: Empty dicts/None for unspecified parameters - **Immutable**: `frozen=True` to prevent test contamination
### __init__.py (Module Export) **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/shell/__init__.py`
```python """Shell detection and tool availability operations.
Import from submodules: - erk_shared.gateway.shell.abc: Shell, detect_shell_from_env - erk_shared.gateway.shell.fake: FakeShell, SpawnSubshellCall - erk_shared.gateway.shell.real: RealShell """ ```
**Key patterns:** - Documents where to import from - Encourages explicit submodule imports - Guides developers to the right implementation
---
## 2. Subprocess Utilities: Integration Pattern
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/subprocess_utils.py`
This is how Real implementations wrap subprocess calls:
```python def run_subprocess_with_context( *, cmd: Sequence[str], operation_context: str, # Human-readable description cwd: Path | None = None, timeout: float | None = None, capture_output: bool = True, text: bool = True, encoding: str = "utf-8", check: bool = True, stdout: int | IO[Any] | None = None, stderr: int | IO[Any] | None = None, **kwargs: Any, ) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]: """Execute subprocess with enriched error reporting.
Wraps subprocess.run() to catch CalledProcessError and re-raise as RuntimeError with operation context, stderr output, and command details. """ timing_desc = _build_timing_description(cmd) start_time = time.perf_counter() logger.debug("Starting: %s", timing_desc)
try: result = subprocess.run( cmd, cwd=cwd, timeout=timeout, capture_output=capture_output, text=text, encoding=encoding, check=check, stdout=stdout, stderr=stderr, **kwargs, ) elapsed_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - start_time) * 1000) logger.debug("Completed in %dms: %s", elapsed_ms, timing_desc) return result
except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as e: cmd_str = " ".join(str(arg) for arg in cmd) error_msg = f"Timed out after {timeout}s trying to {operation_context}" error_msg += f"\nCommand: {cmd_str}" raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e
except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: cmd_str = " ".join(str(arg) for arg in cmd) error_msg = f"Failed to {operation_context}" error_msg += f"\nCommand: {cmd_str}" error_msg += f"\nExit code: {e.returncode}" if e.stdout: stdout_text = e.stdout if isinstance(e.stdout, str) else e.stdout.decode("utf-8") error_msg += f"\nstdout: {stdout_text.strip()}" if e.stderr: stderr_text = e.stderr if isinstance(e.stderr, str) else e.stderr.decode("utf-8") error_msg += f"\nstderr: {stderr_text.strip()}" raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e
except FileNotFoundError as e: cmd_str = " ".join(str(arg) for arg in cmd) error_msg = f"Command not found while trying to {operation_context}: {cmd[0]}" error_msg += f"\nFull command: {cmd_str}" raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e ```
**Key patterns:** - **Operation context**: Human-readable description for error messages - **Error enrichment**: Adds command, exit code, stdout/stderr to errors - **Timing logs**: Tracks performance at DEBUG level - **Consistent error type**: Everything becomes RuntimeError for consistent handling - **Type preservation**: Returns `CompletedProcess[str]` for text mode
---
## 3. ErkContext: Dependency Injection Hub
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py`
### ErkContext dataclass (excerpt) ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class ErkContext: """Immutable context holding all dependencies."""
# Gateway integrations git: Git github: LocalGitHub github_admin: GitHubAdmin graphite: Graphite graphite_branch_ops: GraphiteBranchOps | None console: Console time: Time erk_installation: ErkInstallation claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation agent_docs: AgentDocs plan_store: PlanBackend prompt_executor: PromptExecutor
# Shell/CLI integrations shell: Shell completion: Completion codespace: Codespace cmux: Cmux agent_launcher: AgentLauncher
# Paths and configuration cwd: Path repo: RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel repo_info: RepoInfo | None global_config: GlobalConfig | None local_config: LoadedConfig
# Optional fields package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None health_check_runner: HealthCheckRunner | None = None http_client: HttpClient | None = None remote_github: RemoteGitHub | None = None
# Mode flags dry_run: bool = False debug: bool = False ```
### for_test() factory method ```python @staticmethod def for_test( *, github_issues: GitHubIssues | None = None, git: Git | None = None, github: LocalGitHub | None = None, github_admin: GitHubAdmin | None = None, claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation | None = None, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None = None, plan_store: PlanBackend | None = None, cmux: Cmux | None = None, remote_github: RemoteGitHub | None = None, debug: bool = False, repo_root: Path | None = None, cwd: Path | None = None, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None, ) -> ErkContext: """Create test context with optional pre-configured implementations.
Provides full control over all context parameters with sensible test defaults for any unspecified values. Uses fakes by default to avoid subprocess calls. """ from erk_shared.context.testing import context_for_test
return context_for_test( github_issues=github_issues, git=git, github=github, github_admin=github_admin, claude_installation=claude_installation, prompt_executor=prompt_executor, plan_store=plan_store, cmux=cmux, remote_github=remote_github, debug=debug, repo_root=repo_root, cwd=cwd, repo_info=repo_info, package_info=package_info, ) ```
**Key patterns:** - **All dependencies injected**: Constructor accepts all gateways - **Frozen dataclass**: Immutable after creation - **Optional parameters**: Only required gateways are mandatory - **Factory method**: `for_test()` delegates to `context_for_test()` for defaults - **Sensible test defaults**: Fakes provided for any unspecified gateways
---
## 4. Production Wiring: ErkContext in erk CLI
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py` (excerpt)
Shows how production code wires Real implementations:
```python # Import Real implementations from erk_shared.gateway.git.real import RealGit from erk_shared.gateway.github.real import RealLocalGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.real import RealGraphite from erk_shared.gateway.shell.abc import Shell # Import ABC from erk_shared.gateway.time.real import RealTime
# Wire in production context factory def create_context(...) -> ErkContext: # Create Real instances git = RealGit(time=time_impl) github = RealLocalGitHub(time=time_impl, repo_info=repo_info) graphite = RealGraphite() or GraphiteDisabled(...) shell = RealShell() # Production implementation time_impl = RealTime()
# Wrap with dry-run if needed if dry_run: git = DryRunGit(wrapped=git) github = DryRunLocalGitHub(wrapped=github) graphite = DryRunGraphite(wrapped=graphite)
# Assemble context return ErkContext( git=git, github=github, graphite=graphite, shell=shell, time=time_impl, # ... other gateways ) ```
**Key patterns:** - **Real* imports**: Import from `.real` module - **ABC type**: Context field typed as ABC (`shell: Shell`) - **Runtime assembly**: Real instances created in factory - **Dry-run wrapping**: Applied AFTER Real creation - **Fallback handling**: GraphiteDisabled sentinel for missing Graphite
---
## 5. Test Infrastructure
### Test Pattern: FakeShell Usage
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_shell.py`
```python def test_fake_shell_ops_detect_shell() -> None: """Test that detect_shell returns pre-configured shell type.""" shell_config = ("bash", Path.home() / ".bashrc") ops = FakeShell(detected_shell=shell_config)
result = ops.detect_shell()
assert result == ("bash", Path.home() / ".bashrc")
def test_fake_shell_ops_get_installed_tool_path() -> None: """Test get_installed_tool_path returns configured tool path.""" ops = FakeShell(installed_tools={"gt": "/usr/local/bin/gt"})
result = ops.get_installed_tool_path("gt")
assert result == "/usr/local/bin/gt"
def test_fake_shell_spawn_subshell() -> None: """Record spawn_subshell call and return configured exit code.""" ops = FakeShell(subshell_exit_code=42)
exit_code = ops.spawn_subshell( cwd=Path("/tmp"), shell_path="/bin/bash", command="echo hello", env={}, )
assert exit_code == 42 assert len(ops.subshell_calls) == 1 assert ops.subshell_calls[0].command == "echo hello" ```
### Integration Test Pattern
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/integration/test_shell.py`
```python def test_real_shell_ops_detect_shell_with_current_environment(): """Test RealShell.detect_shell() works with actual environment.""" ops = RealShell() result = ops.detect_shell()
# Validates against actual SHELL environment variable shell_env = os.environ.get("SHELL", "") if shell_env and any(s in shell_env for s in ["bash", "zsh", "fish"]): assert result is not None shell_name, rc_file = result assert shell_name in ["bash", "zsh", "fish"]
def test_real_shell_ops_get_installed_tool_path(): """Test checking if a tool is installed.""" ops = RealShell()
# Check for a tool that should always exist on Unix systems result = ops.get_installed_tool_path("sh") assert result is not None # sh should always exist
# Check for a tool that likely doesn't exist result = ops.get_installed_tool_path("nonexistent-tool-xyz-123") assert result is None ```
**Key patterns:** - **Unit tests** (tests/unit/fakes/): Test the fake itself - **Integration tests** (tests/integration/): Test Real with actual system - **Constructor injection**: All test data passed to constructor - **Mutation tracking properties**: For assertions on what was called
---
## 6. 3-File vs 4-File Gateway Patterns
From the documentation and code:
### 3-File Pattern (Default) ``` gateway/shell/ ├── __init__.py # Public exports ├── abc.py # Abstract interface ├── real.py # Production implementation └── fake.py # Test double ```
**Used for:** Shell, Cmux, Codespace, Console, Time, Completion, etc. **When to use:** All new gateways unless participating in `--dry-run` feature.
### 4-File Pattern (Git, GitHub, Graphite, AgentDocs) ``` gateway/git/ ├── __init__.py # Public exports ├── abc.py # Abstract interface ├── real.py # Production implementation ├── fake.py # Test double └── dry_run.py # Dry-run wrapper (ONLY for --dry-run features) ```
**Used for:** Git, LocalGitHub, Graphite, AgentDocs **When to use:** ONLY when the gateway participates in CLI `--dry-run` feature.
**Dry-run pattern:** ```python class DryRunShell(Shell): """Dry-run wrapper - no changes to actual system."""
def __init__(self, wrapped: Shell) -> None: self._wrapped = wrapped
# Read-only methods: delegate def detect_shell(self) -> tuple[str, Path] | None: return self._wrapped.detect_shell()
# Mutation methods: no-op def spawn_subshell(...) -> int: return 0 # No-op, return success ```
---
## Summary: Core Gateway Principles
1. **One ABC, Three+ Implementations** - `abc.py`: Abstract contract with docstrings - `real.py`: Production with subprocess/API calls - `fake.py`: Test double with constructor injection - `dry_run.py`: (Optional) Safety preview mode
2. **Constructor Injection Pattern** - All state passed to `__init__` - No globals or environment vars read in constructor - Fakes accept test data via constructor parameters
3. **Mutation Tracking for Fakes** - Internal lists/dicts to record calls - Read-only `@property` to return copies for assertions - Never mutable after construction (`frozen=True` for records)
4. **ErkContext: The DI Container** - Holds all gateway instances - `for_test()` factory with sensible defaults - Production context wires Real implementations - Tests inject fakes via constructor
5. **Error Handling Pattern** - Use `run_subprocess_with_context()` for subprocess calls - Wraps errors with operation context + command + output - Converts all subprocess errors to RuntimeError
6. **Dependency Wiring** - ABC imported at module level - Real/Fake injected at context construction - No circular imports (use callbacks if needed)
7. **Testing Layers** - Unit tests (tests/unit/fakes/): Test the fakes themselves - Integration tests (tests/integration/): Test Real with actual system - Command tests (tests/commands/): Test CLI using injected fakes - Core tests (tests/core/): Test business logic using fakes
These patterns enable complete testability without mocks, full dry-run support, and clean separation of concerns across the erk codebase.
